title: Tuberculosis Case Detection And Treatment Completion Gaps In High-Burden Countries
summary: Build a verified workflow for measuring the TB detection gap and treatment completion gap in high-burden countries, linking national TB program data, prevalence surveys, and private-sector care data at sub-national scale.
why_it_matters: TB killed 1.3 million people in 2023, making it the leading infectious killer after COVID-19. WHO estimates 4 million of the 10.6 million incident TB cases were undiagnosed or unreported. The private sector treats up to 50 percent of TB patients in several high-burden countries but does not systematically report to national programs. Sub-national detection gap measurement that accounts for private-sector treatment is the missing piece for targeting active case finding.

success_metrics.target: Every detection-gap estimate links notification, prevalence, and private-sector data with methodology.

review_policy.merge_gate: Schema validation, domain review, safety review, replication.
